title: fraxinus anomala var. anomala
intro: Fraxinus anomala var. anomala, also known as the Manchurian Ash, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, belonging to the olive family (Oleaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which are believed to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Fraxinus anomala var. anomala include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
